channel.title: agree or disagree on My New Hugo Site
channel.description: Recent content in agree or disagree on My New Hugo Site

item.title: Extracurricular activities are just a distraction
item.description: Extracurricular activities are just a distraction from academic activities and bring students very few actual benefits. How far do you agree with this statement? Support your point of view with reasons and/or examples from your own experience. In today’s world, designing a good school curriculum has been a heated discussion. Some educators think there are few benefits gained from extracurricular activities while these activities put extra burden on students.

item.title: Older professional people to retrain as school teachers
item.description: In some countries, there have been schemes to encourage older professional people to retrain as school teachers. Do you feel that this is likely to have beneficial effects on education? Support your point of view with reasons and/or examples from your own experience. In many countries, the scarcity of high quality teachers has put great pressure on the education system. In these countries, educational strategists come up with schemes to encourage senior members in the workforce to teach at schools.

item.title: Vaccinating Children
item.description: Vaccinating children against preventable diseases is not only unnecessary but also dangerous. To what extend do you agree or disagree? Vaccinating children is discouraged among certain demographics in society. These people claim that vaccinations are unsafe for children’s health and as such are not needed. This essay will discuss why vaccinating against widespread diseases are extremely vital to the health of children and society, despite some potential risks.

item.title: Smacking Children
item.description: Smacking children is the best form of discipline. To what extent do you agree or disagree? Using physical punishment in raising children is commonplace in some demographics in society. These people believe that smacking children is the most effective means to maintain a high standard of discipline. This essay will argue why violent methods are unnecessary despite some reasonable explanations. Smacking children has long been proven to be an effective way of getting children behaved.
